sanskrit: रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम्। वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम्॥
transliteration: rudrāṇāṁ śaṅkaraś cāsmi vitteśo yakṣa-rakṣasām | vasūnāṁ pāvakaś cāsmi meruḥ śikhariṇām aham
literal_meaning: Among Rudras I am Śaṅkara; among Yakṣas and Rākṣasas I am Kuvera; among Vasus I am Agni; among mountains I am Meru.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the apex archetype within each category, showing that every psychological subsystem has a ‘peak’ that points back to the Self. Śaṅkara is the calm center of destructive power; Kuvera the healthy use of resources; Agni the transformative fire; Meru the stable core. Identifying with the summit collapses scattered identities into one coherent center.
psychological_state: Post-fragmentation integration; the mind gathers scattered sub-personalities under one symbolic umbrella, reducing inner noise.
trigger_scenario: After a burnout you list every role you play—manager, parent, athlete, gamer, caretaker—and suddenly see one coherent identity underneath them all.
anchor_text: Find the calm summit in every part of you and let it anchor your whole self.
